title: dasymaschalon rostratum var. glaucum
intro: Dasymaschalon rostratum var. glaucum, also known as the blue-fruited dasymaschalon, is a species of plant in the Annonaceae family, native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Dasymaschalon rostratum var. glaucum include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically prepared from the plant's roots and leaves.
